note1: number of LinearPerMetapath projection layers inside PaperTower. Each layer transforms the metapath features through a learned weight matrix.
note3: randomly zeros 30% of activations during training inside PaperTower to prevent overfitting. Disabled at evaluation time
note4: randomly zeros 10% of input features during training inside PaperTower to prevent overfitting. Disabled at evaluation time
note5: scales the similarity scores in the InfoNCE loss. Lower value makes the loss sharper and more sensitive to small differences between positive and negative pairs. 0.07 is the standard value from contrastive learning literature.
note6: maximum number of tokens SciBERT reads from each citation context
note7: learning rate for SciBERT weights (verry low). Very small because SciBERT is already pretrained on scientific text —> we fine-tune it gently rather than retrain it
note8: learning rate for the projection head weights. Higher because these weights are randomly initialized and need to learn quickly.
note9: learning rate for the PaperTower weights. Higher because these weights are randomly initialized and need to learn quickly.
note10: fixed random seed generator for reproducibility
note11: index of the GPU to use for training. 0 means the first GPU in the system.
note12: the size of the final output vector from both towers
note2: hidden is the dimension of the vector that represents each token after passing through the transformer layers in the paperTower and not the context tower because context tower uses SciBERT which has a fixed hidden size of 768.
